page_title: Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks | Your browser, brilliantly organized
page_desc: Separate contexts with Workspaces, jump anywhere with Spotlight — the ultimate Chrome sidebar combining vertical tabs, bookmarks, private AI and cross-device sync.
hero_subtitle:
Separate every context with Workspaces, and reach any tab, bookmark or action with one shortcut (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+K).
Combined with vertical tabs, seamless bookmarks and 100% on-device AI, build a command center that syncs across your devices.
features_subtitle: We've integrated all the tools you need into one elegant sidebar.
ai_desc: When you have dozens of tabs and don't know where to start, click a button, and AI will categorize them for you. Best of all, everything runs on your device via Gemini Nano. Data never leaves your machine.
drag_desc: We blurred the line between tabs and bookmarks. Drag a tab into a folder, and it becomes a bookmark. Pull a bookmark out, and it becomes an active tab.
theme_desc: Built-in beautiful themes, custom HSL colors support, and even custom background image uploads to build your unique sidebar style.
reading_desc: Don't let "read it later" become "never read it". A built-in article stash focused on information absorption.
rss_desc: Automatically fetch updates from your favorite tracking sites without installing extra readers.
privacy_desc: We believe great tools shouldn't cost you your privacy. That's why we use Chrome's built-in LanguageModel API to run AI grouping 100% locally on your device, instead of sending your every move to an external server.
footer_copy: © 2024-2026 Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks. All rights reserved.
more_subtitle: From search to keyboard control, every detail has been polished.
linked_desc: Opening a bookmark from the sidebar automatically creates a link, avoiding duplicate tabs and syncing state bidirectionally.
cross_window_desc: View and search tabs across all open Chrome windows, not just the current one.
search_desc: Multi-keyword filtering, domain search, and smart real-time highlighting—find anything instantly.
keyboard_desc: Arrow keys for navigation, F2 to rename, Delete to remove, Cmd/Ctrl+I to toggle the sidebar.
setup_subtitle: Just three steps to unlock AI smart grouping on your device.
setup_step1_desc: Navigate to chrome://flags/#optimization-guide-on-device-model and set it to "Enabled".
setup_step2_desc: Navigate to chrome://flags/#prompt-api-for-gemini-nano and set it to "Enabled" or "Enabled Multilingual".
setup_step3_desc: Restart Chrome, then go to chrome://on-device-internals to verify the model download. You can also check chrome://components for "Optimization Guide On Device Model" download progress.
setup_note: The official docs list English, Spanish, and Japanese as supported languages, but other languages actually work as well. If your language isn't on the official list, feel free to give it a try!
privacy_effective_date: Effective Date: February 10, 2026 (Last updated: June 8, 2026 — added a “Google User Data Disclosure” section covering the five disclosures required by the Google API Services User Data Policy)
privacy_intro: Thank you for using the “Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks” Chrome extension (hereinafter referred to as “the Extension”). We are committed to protecting your personal privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle your information.
privacy_s1_desc: To provide its core functionality, the Extension requests read permissions for specific data from the Chrome browser. The types of information we access include:
privacy_s1_li1: Tabs and Tab Groups: URLs and titles of your open tabs, and titles/colors of tab groups.
privacy_s1_li2: Bookmarks: URLs, titles, and folder structure of all your bookmarks.
privacy_s1_li3: Reading List: URLs, titles, and read status of items in your Chrome Reading List.
privacy_s1_li4: Operational Data: State of “Linked Tabs”, UI state, RSS subscriptions, and hashes of fetched articles.
privacy_s2_desc: We follow the principle of data minimization and use different storage methods based on data type:
privacy_s2_li1: Local Data: Core operational data such as your tabs, bookmarks, Linked Tabs state, and hashes of fetched RSS articles are stored only in your device's chrome.storage.local. Apart from the optional feature described in “2.1 Workspace Google Drive Sync” below, we do not upload this data to any developer or third-party servers.
privacy_s2_li2: Settings Sync: To provide a consistent experience, your preferences (e.g., themes, RSS list) are stored in chrome.storage.sync and synced securely via your Google account.
privacy_s2_1_title: 2.1 Workspace Google Drive Sync (optional, off by default)
privacy_s2_1_desc: To let you sync and restore the tabs in a “Workspace” across multiple devices, the Extension offers an opt-in Google Drive sync feature. Its privacy design is as follows:
privacy_s2_1_li1: Off by default, per-workspace opt-in: This feature is off by default. Nothing is uploaded unless you actively connect Google Drive and enable sync for a specific workspace. Workspaces without sync enabled behave exactly as before (local only).
privacy_s2_1_li2: What is uploaded: For workspaces you have enabled sync for, we upload that workspace's tab snapshot, containing: each tab's URL, title, pinned state, and the tab-group name and color. This is the first time the Extension transmits full tab URLs and titles off your device, so we disclose it explicitly here.
privacy_s2_1_li3: Where it is stored: This data is written only to your own Google Drive, in the app-private hidden folder appDataFolder (accessed via the drive.appdata OAuth scope). This folder is app-private: only this Extension can access it, it is invisible in your regular Drive file list, and it is automatically deleted by Google when you uninstall the Extension. This data is never transmitted to the developer's servers or any third-party server.
privacy_s2_1_li4: Authorization: We obtain access to your Drive appDataFolder via Chrome's built-in chrome.identity API; no long-lived credentials are stored. You can click “Disconnect Google Drive” at any time to immediately stop all uploads and clear cached access tokens.
privacy_s2_1_li5: Stopping uploads and deleting your data: Disabling sync for a specific workspace only stops further uploads; it does not automatically delete that workspace's already-uploaded cloud data. To remove uploaded cloud data you can: disconnect Google Drive, delete the workspace (a deletion marker is written to your Drive so it is removed across your devices), remove this app's data via Google Drive's “Manage apps”, or uninstall the Extension (Google then deletes everything in appDataFolder).
privacy_limited_use: Google API Services User Data Policy (Limited Use): The use of information received from Google APIs will adhere to the Chrome Web Store User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.
privacy_s2_2_title: 2.2 Google User Data Disclosure (Google Drive API)
privacy_s2_2_desc: In line with the Google API Services User Data Policy, the following discloses — across the five areas that policy requires — exactly how the Extension (Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks) accesses, uses, stores, and shares Google user data:
privacy_s2_2_accessed: Data Accessed: The Extension requests a single OAuth scope, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.appdata, which grants access only to a hidden, application-private folder (appDataFolder) that the Extension itself creates in your Google Drive. The Extension does not access any of your existing Drive files, documents, photos, or folders, and does not access your Google profile, email address, or contacts.
privacy_s2_2_usage: Data Usage: The accessed data is limited to the tab snapshots of the workspaces you have explicitly enabled sync for (each tab’s URL, title, and pinned state, plus tab-group names and colors) and a small index file. This data is used solely to synchronize and restore your workspaces across your own devices. It is never used for advertising, profiling, analytics, or AI/ML model training.
privacy_s2_2_sharing: Data Sharing: The Extension does not share this data with any third party. It flows only between your device(s) and your own Google Drive via the official Google Drive API, and is never transmitted to the developer’s servers or any other server — the Extension has no backend server.
privacy_s2_2_storage: Data Storage & Protection: The data resides only in your own Google Drive’s application-private appDataFolder, protected by your Google account’s security; it is encrypted in transit with HTTPS/TLS. The Extension stores no long-lived credentials: authorization tokens are managed by Chrome’s built-in chrome.identity layer, and short-lived access tokens are never persisted by the Extension. The developer operates no server and therefore never receives or holds this data.
privacy_s2_2_retention: Data Retention & Deletion: The data remains in your Drive until you delete it. You can delete it at any time by: (a) clicking “Disconnect Google Drive” in the Extension’s settings; (b) deleting the synced workspace (a deletion marker is written so it is removed across your devices); (c) removing this app’s data via Google Drive’s “Settings → Manage apps”; or (d) uninstalling the Extension (Google then automatically deletes the entire appDataFolder). For any data-deletion request or question, contact tai@taislife.work.
privacy_s3_desc: The Extension generally does not transmit your personal information externally. Exceptions involving external network requests are:
privacy_s3_li1: Bookmark Favicons: To display website icons, we send the domain (e.g., google.com) to Google's public Favicon service. We never transmit the full URL containing personal paths or parameters.
privacy_s3_li2: RSS Feed Fetching: The Extension periodically sends HTTP requests to your subscribed RSS feed URLs. These requests are limited to the URLs you actively subscribe to, and fetched content is only added to your Reading List, never sent to third-party servers.
privacy_s4_desc: The Extension uses the following Chrome permissions:
privacy_s4_td1: Read tab information to display and manage tabs in the sidebar.
privacy_s4_td2: Read and manage titles and colors of tab groups.
privacy_s4_td3: Read and manage the bookmark list.
privacy_s4_td5: Store extension settings and operational state.
privacy_s4_td6: Manage Chrome Reading List (add/remove/mark as read).
privacy_s4_td7: Schedule background fetching tasks for RSS subscriptions.
privacy_s4_td8: Process XML parsing of RSS feeds in the background.
privacy_s4_td9: Used to fetch RSS feed content and display bookmark favicons.
privacy_s5_desc: All accessed information is strictly used for:
privacy_s5_li1: Rendering and presenting your tabs and bookmarks in the sidebar.
privacy_s5_li2: Enabling management operations (searching, sorting, grouping, closing, deleting).
privacy_s5_li3: Managing your Chrome Reading List and RSS feed content.
privacy_s5_outro: Your data will never be used for advertising, tracking, or other analytical purposes.
privacy_s6_desc: This extension is 100% open-source software under the MIT License. We welcome anyone to review our code to verify this Privacy Policy.
privacy_s7_desc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page.
privacy_s8_desc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact:
terms_page_title: Terms of Service | Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks
terms_intro: Welcome to the “Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks” Chrome extension (hereinafter referred to as “the Extension”). These Terms of Service (hereinafter referred to as “these Terms”) constitute an agreement between you and the developer. By installing or using the Extension, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by all of these Terms.
terms_s1_desc: Installing or using the Extension constitutes your acceptance of these Terms. If you do not agree to any part of these Terms, please do not install or use the Extension. These Terms should be read together with our Privacy Policy.
terms_s2_desc: The Extension is a free Chrome browser sidebar tool that provides vertical tabs, bookmark management, a reading list, RSS subscriptions, optional on-device AI grouping, and optional Workspace Google Drive sync. The Extension is provided free of charge as is, and we may add, modify, or remove any feature at any time without prior notice.
terms_s3_desc: The Extension is open-source software, with its source code released under the MIT License. You may use, copy, modify, and distribute the software in accordance with the terms of that license. The full license is available in the LICENSE file on GitHub.
terms_s4_desc: When using the Extension, you agree to:
terms_s4_li1: Comply with all applicable laws and regulations, and not use the Extension for any illegal or unauthorized purpose.
terms_s4_li2: Be responsible for the security of your own data (including tabs, bookmarks, and workspace contents) and of any Google account you connect.
terms_s4_li3: Not engage in any activity that may interfere with, disrupt the normal operation of, or circumvent the security mechanisms of the Extension.
terms_s5_desc: Some optional features of the Extension interact with third-party services. When using these features, you are also bound by the terms of service of those third parties:
terms_s5_li1: Google Drive Sync (optional): If you choose to enable Workspace sync, the relevant data is stored in your own Google Drive. This feature is subject to the Google Terms of Service. For details on how data is handled, please see our Privacy Policy.
terms_s5_li2: Favicons and RSS: The Extension requests website icons from Google's public Favicon service and sends requests to the RSS sources you actively subscribe to. We are not responsible for the availability or content of these third-party services.
terms_s6_desc: Your use of the Extension is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which explains how we handle your information. We adhere to the principle of data minimization: core data is stored only on your device, and Workspace sync writes data to your own Google Drive only when you actively enable it.
terms_s7_desc: The Extension is provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis, without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. We do not warrant that the Extension will be uninterrupted, error-free, secure, or that it will meet your specific requirements.
terms_s8_desc: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the developer shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages arising from the use of or inability to use the Extension — including but not limited to loss or corruption of data (such as tabs, bookmarks, or workspace data). The Extension is provided free of charge, and you should keep your own backups of important data.
terms_s9_title: 9. Changes to the Service and Terms
terms_s9_desc: We reserve the right to modify or discontinue the Extension (in whole or in part) at any time. We may also update these Terms from time to time, and any changes will be posted on this page. Your continued use of the Extension after such changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
terms_s10_desc: You may terminate these Terms at any time by removing the Extension via Chrome. After removal, the local data stored on your device will be cleared; if you have used Google Drive sync, Google automatically deletes the data in the app-private folder (appDataFolder) upon uninstallation.
terms_s11_desc: To the extent permitted by applicable law, these Terms shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the laws of the developer's location (the Republic of China / Taiwan), without regard to its conflict-of-law principles.
terms_s12_desc: If you have any questions about these Terms, please feel free to contact me:
changelog_subtitle: See what's new, improved, and fixed in recent updates.
changelog_read_more: Read full release notes on GitHub
v1_13_3_desc: Fixed an issue where background RSS fetching would occasionally drop connections, ensuring your feeds are updated reliably. We also optimized the way background files are packaged for better performance.
v1_13_2_desc: Enhanced our RSS fetcher to smoothly support more websites, including Substack. Your information flow is now less restricted than ever.
v1_13_1_desc: We solved a "failed to load" error that occurred when subscribing to new RSS feeds from the sidebar. We've also massively upgraded bookmark rendering performance, making list scrolling buttery smooth, alongside crucial UI security patches.
v1_14_0_desc: Massive update! Introducing the experimental 'Local AI Auto-Grouping' to organize your tabs with a single click. You can now manually switch the UI language to any of 14 supported languages from the settings! Plus, a completely overhauled official website and multiple UX enhancements including RSS.
guide_subtitle: From installation to advanced control, master every function of the sidebar step by step.
guide_start_step1: Go to the <a href="https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/sidebar-for-tabsbookmarks/beoonblekmppafnjppedgpgfngghebji" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Chrome Web Store
guide_start_step2: After the installation is complete, click on the extension icon (puzzle icon) in the upper right corner of the Chrome toolbar, find "Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks" and pin it.
guide_start_step3: Click the icon or use the shortcut <kbd>Cmd/Ctrl + I
guide_start_tip: Tip: After opening the sidebar for the first time, press any direction key to automatically gain focus and start keyboard navigation.
guide_tabs_desc: The sidebar will display all the tabs of the current window vertically, allowing you to see the complete titles at a glance, instead of being compressed into small icons like the traditional horizontal tab column.
guide_groups_desc: Fully integrates Chrome's native paging group functionality. The name and color of the group will be displayed simultaneously.
guide_bm_desc: A complete bookmark browser is built into the sidebar, which directly displays the structure of your bookmark folders and supports expansion, search and drag operations.
guide_linked_desc: This is our most unique feature! When you open a bookmark from the sidebar, the system will automatically create a "link" between the bookmark and the tab to help you avoid opening it repeatedly and synchronize the status in real time.
guide_linked_tip: The link state is persisted through Local Storage and will not be lost even if the browser is restarted.
guide_window_desc: Not just the current window! The sidebar displays an "Other Windows" section below the pagination list, letting you know the pagination status of all Chrome windows.
guide_search_desc: The search box at the top of the sidebar provides a powerful real-time filtering function, allowing you to instantly lock in your target among dozens of tabs and thousands of bookmarks.
guide_reading_desc: The sidebar deeply integrates Chrome's native reading list, allowing you to manage "read later" articles without switching panels.
guide_rss_desc: No need to install additional readers! Subscribe to the RSS feed of websites you are interested in in the settings panel, and the system will automatically schedule to fetch the latest articles and add them to the reading list.
guide_ai_desc: With Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano native AI, scattered pages can be automatically classified into groups based on semantics with just one click. All processing takes place 100% on your device, and data is never uploaded to the cloud.
guide_ai_step1: Ensure you've completed the prerequisites above (requires Chrome 140+ and 22GB disk space).
guide_ai_step2: Click the "Smart Grouping" button in the sidebar.
guide_ai_step3: All uncategorized tabs (at least 4 required) will be scanned and analyzed by the AI within seconds.
guide_ai_step4: Paginations are automatically moved into groups with emojis and descriptive names.
guide_ai_undo_tip: Not satisfied with the classification results? don’t worry! After the organization is completed, the "Undo" button will be displayed. Click to restore to the state before organization.
guide_theme_desc: The sidebar provides canvas-level visual customization capabilities, from preset themes to fully customized, to create your own unique tool.
guide_theme_preset_desc: A variety of beautiful built-in themes (Geek, Google, Darcula, Geek-Blue, Christmas, etc.) can be switched with one click in the settings.
guide_theme_custom_desc: After selecting the "Custom" theme, you can freely set the three core colors through the color selector - the main background color, accent color and text color. The system will automatically derive a harmonious auxiliary color chip through the HSL algorithm to ensure that the WCAG contrast ratio meets the standard.
guide_theme_share_desc: Export your custom colors as a JSON file to share with friends, or load themes shared by others to quickly apply popular colors in the community.
guide_kb_desc: The sidebar supports a complete keyboard operation experience, allowing advanced users to complete all operations without a mouse.
guide_kb_newtab: Create a new tab next to the current tab
guide_kb_updown: Move focus between tabs, groups, and bookmarked items
guide_kb_leftright: Action buttons for entering/leaving the project (close, edit, etc.)
guide_kb_enter: Perform main actions (switch pagination, expand folders)
guide_kb_focus_tip: Focus Tip: When focus is at the top of the list, press <kbd>↑
guide_cta_desc: Install now and give your Chrome a new look.
hover_summarize_desc: Not sure what a tab is about? Hover your cursor over any loaded tab in the sidebar for 2 seconds. The system will use local AI to read the page content in real-time and generate a concise one-sentence summary.
modern_ui_desc: Deeply polished glassmorphism design and silky micro-animations for the ultimate browsing experience.
compare_h1:
Not More Settings, But
Better Experience
compare_subtitle: We are not aiming to be a 'Swiss Army Knife' with 100+ toggles. We focus on building an out-of-the-box, elegant, and 100% privacy-safe browser sidebar.
compare_row1_us: Zero-config (Built-in Local AI)
compare_row1_allinone: Requires Manual API Key
compare_row1_traditional: No AI Integration
compare_row2_us: Minimalist Arc Style & Micro-animations
compare_row2_allinone: Focus on Feature Density over Visuals
compare_row2_traditional: Native Browser UI
compare_row3_us: 100% Offline Analysis
compare_row3_allinone: Sends Data to 3rd-Party APIs
compare_row3_traditional: Offline
compare_row4_us: Opinionated Best Defaults
compare_row4_allinone: 100+ Settings, Steep Learning Curve
compare_row4_traditional: Simple & Intuitive
compare_row5_us: First-of-its-kind Hover Summaries
compare_row5_allinone: Unsupported
compare_row5_traditional: Unsupported
compare_cta_text: We believe good tools should filter distractions, not fill your screen with unnecessary switches.
compare_btn_install: Install now to feel the difference
guide_ai_hover_desc: Not sure what a tab is about? Hover your cursor over any loaded tab in the sidebar for 2 seconds. The system will use local AI to read the page content in real-time and generate a concise one-sentence summary.
guide_ai_hover_step1: Ensure you have completed the AI Setup.
guide_ai_hover_step2: Enable 'Hover Smart Summarize' in the 'Experimental Features' section of the sidebar's settings panel.
guide_ai_hover_step3: Hover your cursor over any fully loaded tab for 2 seconds.
guide_ai_hover_step4: The summary panel will elegantly appear, streaming the summary text word by word.
guide_ai_hover_lang_limit: Language Support Limitation: Due to the current language support in the underlying Gemini Nano model, this feature can only reliably generate summaries in Japanese, English, and Spanish. Users of other languages will currently receive English summaries. We will support more languages as soon as Chrome rolls out new model updates.
setup_step2b_desc: Navigate to chrome://flags/#prompt-api-for-gemini-nano-multimodal-input and set it to "Enabled".
setup_step4_desc: Open DevTools Console and run await LanguageModel.availability(); — it should return "available". You're all set to use AI features!
guide_ai_setup_desc: Before using the AI features, you need to enable these experimental flags in Chrome:
guide_ai_setup_step2: Navigate to chrome://flags/#prompt-api-for-gemini-nano and set to "Enabled" or "Enabled (Multi-language)".
guide_ai_setup_step4: Go to chrome://on-device-internals to verify model status; you can also check chrome://components for "Optimization Guide On Device Model" download progress.
guide_ai_setup_verify: Verification: Open DevTools Console and run await LanguageModel.availability(); — if it returns "available", setup is complete.
privacy_s4_td_scripting: Used for the "Hover Smart Summary" feature: when you hover over a tab, it extracts the visible text content from that page and sends it to the on-device local AI model for summarization. The extracted text is processed entirely on your device and is never uploaded to any server.
privacy_s4_td_identity: Used for the optional “Workspace Google Drive Sync” feature: obtains authorization to access your own Google Drive appDataFolder (the drive.appdata scope) via Chrome's built-in identity. Used only when you actively connect Google Drive.
alt_workona_sub: Experience a privacy-first, lightweight vertical tab manager that doesnt slow down your browser.
alt_workona_card1_desc: All data and AI categorization is handled locally on your device. We dont read your tabs.
alt_workona_card1_them_desc: Often requires cloud syncing, meaning your tab history and workspaces live on an external server.
alt_workona_card2_desc: Built as a native-feeling Chrome sidebar, it loads instantly and consumes minimal memory.
alt_workona_card2_them_desc: A heavy dashboard replacing the new tab page can slow down your daily workflow.
alt_workona_card3_desc: True Arc-like vertical tabs that sit comfortably on the side of your screen.
alt_workona_card3_them_desc: Workspace manager mainly operating in a dedicated dashboard.
alt_workona_faq1_q: Can I import my tabs from Workona?
alt_workona_faq1_a: Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks uses native Chrome Tab Groups and Bookmarks. If you export Workona workspaces to Chrome bookmarks, they instantly sync with our sidebar!
alt_workona_faq2_q: Is Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks free compared to Workona?
alt_workona_faq2_a: Yes! The core functionality including vertical tabs and reading lists operates completely free of charge without arbitrary workspace limits.
alt_toby_sub: Transform your chaotic tabs into structured collections directly from your sidebar—without taking over your new tab page.
alt_toby_card1_desc: Access your saved tabs and active groups from a sleek sidebar that stays with you on any webpage.
alt_toby_card1_them_desc: Forces you to open a new tab page just to manage or view your collections.
alt_toby_card2_desc: Includes on-device Gemini Nano AI to automatically read and group your messy tabs with one click.
alt_toby_card2_them_desc: Manual dragging and dropping is required to organize your spaces.
alt_toby_card3_desc: Operates directly on standard Chrome Bookmarks and Tab Groups, so your data is always synced via your Google Account without third-party lock-in.
alt_toby_card3_them_desc: Saves your links in a proprietary database format.
alt_toby_faq1_q: Will this replace my new tab page like Toby does?
alt_toby_faq1_a: No! Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks respects your browser habits. It runs as a sidebar (Side Panel API) so you can keep whatever new tab page you already love.
alt_toby_faq2_q: Can I use both at the same time?
alt_toby_faq2_a: Yes, absolutely. Since Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks uses native Chrome APIs, it wont conflict with Toby, though you might find you dont need Toby anymore!
alt_vt_sub: Upgrade your browser with the ultimate Arc-inspired sidebar that combines vertical tabs, bookmarks, and AI.
alt_vt_title: Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks vs Standard Vertical Tabs
alt_vt_card1_desc: Manage Vertical Tabs, Chrome Bookmarks, and a Reading List all inside a unified, beautifully designed panel.
alt_vt_card1_them_desc: Usually only provides a list of vertical tabs, requiring separate extensions for bookmarks or reading lists.
alt_vt_card2_desc: Designed meticulously with dynamic themes, glassmorphism, and a UI inspired by the modern Arc Browser experience.
alt_vt_card2_them_desc: Often features outdated UI design that resembles native tree menus.
alt_vt_card3_desc: Hover to summarize page contents instantly or auto-group 50+ tabs by context using completely private, offline AI.
alt_vt_faq1_q: How is this different from Edges native vertical tabs?
alt_vt_faq1_a: While Edge has built-in vertical tabs, Chrome does not. Sidebar for Tabs/Bookmarks brings this highly sought-after feature to Chrome, but supercharges it by mixing in your bookmarks and offline AI.
alt_vt_faq2_q: Does it support nested tree-style tabs?
alt_vt_faq2_a: We focus on a cleaner, Arc-style approach using Chromes native Tab Groups rather than deep infinite trees, keeping your sidebar clutter-free and fast.
alternatives_section_title: Looking for the best alternatives to other solutions?
v1_14_1_desc: Added a comprehensive user guide page with a redesigned navbar, integrated official website links across the codebase and docs, and set up GitHub Actions for automated release builds.
v1_14_2_desc: Focused improvements on AI features and security: added a detailed Gemini Nano setup guide with best practices, improved hover summary caching with TTL and progress bar visibility. Also addressed multiple CodeQL security alerts and significantly improved localization quality across all languages.
v1_15_0_desc: A big visual and feature update. New unified Spotlight search — press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+K anywhere to instantly find tabs, bookmarks, reading list and actions in a centered popup. Optional Google Drive sync keeps your Workspaces across devices. Plus a full Material 3 visual refresh: all icons redrawn as SVGs, a streamlined single-button workspace bar, adjustable list spacing, unified scrollbars and consistent theme colors — along with many UI fixes.
v1_14_3_desc: Added a confirmation dialog for custom theme reset to prevent accidental loss; fixed scroll position jumping when clicking tabs after scrolling; added accessibility labels to the toast close button; fixed missing i18n keys across all locales; and updated multiple dev dependencies.
workspace_desc: Create separate Workspaces for work, study and life, and switch your tab & bookmark layout for each context with one click. Enable optional Google Drive sync to keep your Workspaces consistent across multiple devices.
spotlight_desc: Press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+K anywhere to summon a centered search popup and jump straight to tabs, bookmarks, reading list and actions.
guide_ws_desc: Workspaces let you group your tab and bookmark layouts by context — for example, separate "Work", "Study" and "Life" setups. When you switch Workspaces, the sidebar applies the layout for that context so you can focus on the task at hand.
guide_ws_li1: Switch workspaces: Click the workspace button at the top of the sidebar to open the management panel and switch between Workspaces.
guide_ws_li2: Create & name: Add a workspace in the management panel, give it a name and pick a representative icon.
guide_ws_li3: Rename / delete: Each workspace row has edit and delete buttons at the end, so you can adjust them anytime.
guide_ws_sync_desc: Want to share the same Workspaces across multiple devices? Connect your Google account on the options page to enable optional Google Drive sync.
guide_ws_sync_li1: Optional and manually enabled: It only starts after you actively click "Connect" on the options page and sign in with your Google account; while disabled, the sync layer stays completely inert.
guide_ws_sync_li2: Minimal scope: It only uses Drive's app data folder (drive.appdata) and cannot read or modify any of your other Drive files.
guide_ws_sync_li3: Disconnect anytime: Disconnecting on the options page signs you out and revokes access; the next connect shows the account picker again.
guide_ws_privacy: Your workspace data is stored only in your own private app-data folder on Google Drive — it never passes through any of our servers.
guide_spotlight_desc: Spotlight is a centered pop-up search window that lets you instantly search and jump to your target from any screen, without first focusing the sidebar.
guide_spotlight_li1: Summon anywhere: Press Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + K in any window to open it.
guide_spotlight_li2: Unified results: Search tabs, bookmarks, reading list and runnable actions all at once, with results updating in real time.
guide_spotlight_li3: Keyboard-first: Select with the arrow keys, run with Enter and close with Esc — no need to leave the keyboard.
guide_spotlight_li4: Quick actions: Beyond opening tabs, you can trigger common actions like Smart Grouping directly.
guide_spotlight_tip: Spotlight appears as its own centered window, so it won't show up in the sidebar's "Other Windows" list.
guide_theme_density_desc: Find the list too loose or too cramped? Adjust the list spacing (density) in the settings so the row spacing of tabs and bookmarks matches your preference — show more items on one screen or keep more breathing room.
